ai_strategy_lead.name: AI at NDSU
ai_strategy_lead.title: Public AI strategy or initiative lead / hub identified from cited sources

ai_strategy.summary: North Dakota State University has a public AI posture anchored in AI at NDSU. The cited sources show activity across responsible-use guidance, teaching resources, research or infrastructure, and AI literacy or workforce preparation.

key_provisions: Use AI transparently and follow course, research, workplace and academic-integrity expectations.
Do not enter confidential, regulated, sensitive, unpublished or protected institutional data into unapproved public AI tools.
Human users remain accountable for accuracy, bias, copyright, privacy and final decisions based on AI output.
ai_policy.summary: North Dakota State University publishes AI policy or guidance with a permissive stance: AI use is allowed or actively explored, but bounded by privacy, ethics, academic integrity, compliance and human-review expectations.

governance_structure.summary: AI governance at North Dakota State University is evidenced through AI at NDSU and the cited policy or strategy sources. Public sources did not identify a separate chief AI officer during this sweep.

initiatives.title: AI SUSTEIN minority-serving research pipeline
initiatives.description: NDSU leads a nearly $6 million NSF EPSCoR Track-2 AI SUSTEIN project with Arkansas, UNLV, and Nueta Hidatsa Sahnish College to advance AI for sustainable energy infrastructure. Public materials emphasize early-career faculty, graduate and undergraduate participation, including Native American, Hispanic, tribal-college, MSI, and HSI participants.
initiatives.significance: The project supplies a concrete student-subgroup and regional-workforce AI signal beyond generic AI literacy programming.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in cited source.

initiatives.policy_implications: Places AI use under instructor-level syllabus expectations and authentic assessment design.
initiatives.title: Generative AI authentic assessment guidance
initiatives.description: NDSU course-design guidance tells instructors to redesign assessments toward process, authentic tasks, oral assessment, personalization, collaboration, critical thinking, and reflective writing in response to GenAI.
initiatives.significance: This closes an assessment-redesign signal with explicit public guidance rather than only generic academic-integrity language.

ai_academic_programs.name: AI teaching, training, degree, certificate or literacy offering

computing_infrastructure.summary: NDSU received a $3.85M NSF MRI award to acquire Bison, an AI-focused supercomputer targeted for initial operation in summer 2026. Public details say Bison will include powerful GPUs and AI-optimized data storage, support more than sixty NDSU AI research groups, and expand access across the North Dakota University System and tribal colleges statewide; exact GPU count and vendor configuration were not disclosed.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Bison statewide and tribal-college compute access
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: NDSU states that Bison will expand advanced computing access to researchers across North Dakota campuses and tribal colleges statewide.

regulatory_context.summary: North Dakota State University operates within a state or system AI-policy context cited in this profile.

narrative_summary: North Dakota State University has moved from generic AI guidance into a clearer research-compute and access posture. The $3.85 million NSF MRI-funded Bison AI supercomputer, targeted for summer 2026 operation, is the strongest capacity signal; NDSU says it will serve more than sixty research groups and extend AI compute access across North Dakota University System campuses and tribal colleges. NDSU also has a notable equity/workforce signal through the nearly $6 million NSF AI SUSTEIN network, which names Native American, Hispanic, tribal-college, MSI, and HSI participation. Teaching evidence includes public guidance on authentic assessment redesign for generative AI. Exact Bison GPU counts, commercial AI platform usage metrics, and post-program student outcomes remain not public.

No campus-wide commercial generative-AI platform license user count or contract value was found.
AI SUSTEIN and Bison sources identify intended subgroup access, but public subgroup outcome metrics are not yet available.
